chunks.text: Agile Design of Efficient Processing Technologies (ADEPT) aims to improve computing by reducing custom silicon design costs and risks through integrated research across applications, programming, architecture, and hardware. Agile Robotics And Perception Lab (ARPL) focuses on robotics autonomy, creating agile machines that navigate unstructured environments using on-board sensors without external infrastructure like GPS. Algorithms and Computing for Education (ACE) Lab researches at the intersection of education and computing, developing educational software for traditional, online, and hybrid learning.

Arts Research Center (ARC) is a think tank for the arts, serving as a hub for artists, scholars, and civic leaders to collaborate. Berkeley Air & Space Center (BASC) advances UC Berkeley's mission in education, research, and public service in air and space fields. Berkeley Artificial Intelligence Research Lab (BAIR) brings together researchers in computer vision, machine learning, NLP, planning, and robotics, focusing on fundamental advances and cross-cutting themes like human-compatible AI.

Berkeley Audio-visual Signal processing and Communication Systems (BASiCS) researches peer-to-peer networks, multimedia processing, and information theory to design robust large-scale systems. Berkeley Benchmarking and Optimization Group (BeBOP) studies software performance tuning and its implications for hardware design, replacing BIPS. Berkeley Center for Computational Imaging (BCCI) focuses on end-to-end imaging system design from sensors to algorithms, impacting areas like MRI and VR.

Berkeley Center for New Media (BCNM) explores new media's impact on perception and communication from cross-disciplinary perspectives. Berkeley Center for Responsible, Decentralized Intelligence (RDI) advances decentralization and web3 science, technology, and education for a responsible digital economy. Berkeley Deep Drive (BDD) integrates deep learning with automotive perception for computer vision in automotive applications.

Berkeley Education Alliance for Research in Singapore (BEARS) focuses on research in power generation, building efficiency, and related technologies. Berkeley Emerging Technologies Research (BETR) Center is a hub for physical electronics research and industry collaborations. Berkeley Equity and Access in Algorithms, Mechanisms, and Optimization (BEAAMO) addresses inequality in algorithms and AI to benefit marginalized communities.

Berkeley Institute for Data Science (BIDS) fosters collaboration across data science communities, including domain and methodological experts. Berkeley Institute of Design (BID) spans computer science, architecture, and engineering disciplines. Berkeley Laboratory for Automation Science and Engineering (AUTOLab) researches robotics and automation in areas like telerobotics and surgery.

Berkeley Laboratory for Information and System Sciences (BLISS) focuses on communications, information theory, optimization, and control. Berkeley Laboratory for Usable and Experimental Security (BLUES) studies computer security and privacy from a human-computer interaction perspective. Berkeley Power and Energy Center (BPEC) promotes research and education in power and energy through industry engagement.

Berkeley Quantum Information and Computation Center (BQIC) explores quantum computing and secure communication. Berkeley Sensor & Actuator Center (BSAC) researches sensors and MEMS using integrated-circuit technology. Berkeley Wireless Research Center (BWRC) focuses on low-energy CMOS implementations and advanced communication algorithms.

Biomimetic Millisystems Lab develops millirobots inspired by animal locomotion. California Institute for Energy and Environment (CIEE) conducts public-interest energy research to meet California's goals. Center for Augmented Cognition (CAC) supports research on human cognition modeling and augmented reality technologies.

Center for Automation and Learning for Medical Robotics (Cal-MR) advances automation and machine learning for medical robots. Center for Computational Biology (CCB) promotes interdisciplinary research at the computational-biological interface. Center for Energy Efficient Electronics Science (E3S) aims to reduce electronic system energy consumption significantly.

Center for Human Compatible Artificial Intelligence (CHAI) reorients AI research towards provably beneficial systems. Center for Information Technology Research in the Interest of Society (CITRIS) sponsors IT research for societal challenges like energy and health. Center for Integrated Access Networks (CIAN) develops transformative optical access network technologies.

Center for Interdisciplinary Biological Inspiration in Education and Research (CiBER) learns from nature to inspire engineering design. Center for Neural Engineering & Prostheses (CNEP) develops technology to restore neurological function. Center for the Theoretical Foundations of Learning, Inference, Information, Intelligence, Mathematics and Microeconomics at Berkeley (CLIMB) addresses interdisciplinary data and decision challenges.

CITRIS Connected Communities enhances communities through IT platforms and systems. CITRIS Health develops IT solutions for health and wellness challenges. CITRIS People and Robots (CPAR) supports robotics research across UC campuses on themes like cloud robotics.

CITRIS Sustainable Infrastructure uses IT to integrate transportation, energy, and water systems for sustainability. CITRIS Women In Tech Initiative (WITI) addresses diversity in the technology industry through research and action. Computer Graphics at Berkeley (B-CAM) focuses on computer animation and modeling.

Electronic Materials Program at LBNL (E-Mat) advances semiconductor materials science for DOE technologies. EPIC Data lab develops low-code interfaces for data work using predictive programming. FHL Vive Center for Enhanced Reality sponsors research in VR, AR, and AI for cross-disciplinary applications.

Foundations Of Resilient CybEr-physical Systems (FORCES) protects critical infrastructure from attacks in areas like energy and transportation. Helen Wills Neuroscience Institute (HWNI) conducts multidisciplinary neuroscience research and the Berkeley BRAIN Initiative. Human-Assistive Robotic Technologies Lab (HART) models human capabilities for developing human-centered robotic systems.

Hyperpolarized MRI Technology Resource Center uses DNP for enhanced MRI imaging of metabolic processes. Industrial Cyber-Physical Systems Center (iCyPhy) develops engineering techniques for complex software-hardware-mechanical products. Industrial Engineering and Operations Research (IEOR) focuses on tools for decision-making in systems like supply chains and healthcare.

Institute of Transportation Studies (ITS) innovates in transportation for sustainability and quality of life. Integrated Modeling Process and Computation for Technology (IMPACT) improves IC manufacturing efficiency through innovative processes. International Computer Science Institute (ICSI) is a nonprofit center for international computer science research collaboration.

Jacobs Institute for Design Innovation (JIDI) is an interdisciplinary hub for design and technology exploration. Kavli Center for Ethics, Science, and the Public (KAVLI) addresses ethical implications of science and technology. Laboratory for Automation Science and Engineering (AUTOLAB) researches robotics and automation in projects like cloud robotics.

Marvell NanoLab provides micro- and nano-fabrication tools for academic and industrial researchers. Multiscale System Center (MuSyC) designs distributed sense and control systems. Networked Systems Lab (NETSYS) addresses networking and systems topics like SDN and data analytics.

NSF Challenge Institute for Quantum Computation (CIQC) focuses on quantum computing challenges like algorithms and scaling. Partners for Advanced Transit and Highways (PATH) develops technologies for improving transportation systems. Quantum Architecture Research explores structures for large-scale quantum computing.

Robotics and Intelligent Machines Laboratory encompasses diverse robotics research from microscopic to autonomous systems. Simons Institute for the Theory of Computing (SITC) promotes fundamental computer science research and interdisciplinary exploration. Sky Computing Lab (Sky) researches cloud computing to decouple applications from specific clouds.

SLAC National Accelerator Laboratory (SLAC) operates major facilities like LCLS and programs in energy and sciences. Software Principles for Advanced Networking (SPAN) aims to make networks more innovative with software-defined techniques. SpeciaLIzed Computing Ecosystems (SLICE) democratizes domain-specific computing design for various applications.

Synthetic Biology Institute (SBI) develops biological systems for applications in energy, materials, and pharmaceuticals. Technology Competitiveness and Industrial Policy Center (TCIP) provides policy advice for technology leadership and economic prosperity. Tele-Immersion enables cooperative interaction in virtual environments for geographically separated groups.

Verified Human Interfaces, Control, and Learning for Semi-Autonomous Systems (VeHICaL) develops verified co-design for human cyber-physical systems. Video and Image Processing Lab (VIP Lab) focuses on signal, image, and video processing theories and applications. Visual Computing Lab (VCL) researches visual computing technologies.
